Jagannathota Prabhala Theertham

# Jagannathota Prabhala Theertham

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Jan 15, 2026

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

5

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

Share

Jagannathota Prabhala Theertham celebrated in Konaseema region of Andhra Pradesh, on Kanuma, the 3rd day of Sankranti here, a very popular festival in this region**.** Jagannapeta is a small hamlet located near Mosalapalli village, Ambajipeta Mandal of Konaseema, where the Prabhala Theertam is celebrated in the coconut groves here. Residents of 90 villages turn up for this celebration.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!MixQ!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbe37da9d-60f2-44b6-a3c9-eafa303b8ca6_3103x2306.jpeg)

![జగ్గన్నతోట ప్రభల తీర్థం - వికీపీడియా](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!MixQ!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbe37da9d-60f2-44b6-a3c9-eafa303b8ca6_3103x2306.jpeg "జగ్గన్నతోట ప్రభల తీర్థం - వికీపీడియా")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

It’s believed that on Kanuma, the Ekadasa Rudras, or the 11 Rudras meet here at Jagannathota, making the occasion a very holy one. The Prabhalu here are the totems carried by devotees,, representing Shiva in different forms.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!cM93!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fdce9cab1-35a1-4ca0-a2a9-3b928256f5a5_1024x764.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!cM93!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fdce9cab1-35a1-4ca0-a2a9-3b928256f5a5_1024x764.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The 11 Rudras or Ekadasha Rudras are Kapali, Pingala, Bheema, Virupaksha, Vilohita, Ajesha, Shasana, Shasta, Shambu.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0a1G!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F912b0d16-fc1b-4802-97de-c23cfa817350_540x329.jpeg)

![Jagganna Thota Prabhala Theertham,11 మంది రుద్రుల కలయిక.. సంక్రాంతికే వన్నెతెచ్చే 'జగ్గన్నతోట ప్రభల తీర్థం' నేడే - sankranti prabhala theertham at jagganna thota in konaseema - Samayam Telugu](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0a1G!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F912b0d16-fc1b-4802-97de-c23cfa817350_540x329.jpeg "Jagganna Thota Prabhala Theertham,11 మంది రుద్రుల కలయిక.. సంక్రాంతికే  వన్నెతెచ్చే 'జగ్గన్నతోట ప్రభల తీర్థం' నేడే - sankranti prabhala theertham  at jagganna thota in konaseema - Samayam Telugu")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

And the Prabhalu are carried from 11 temples here depicting the Rudras.

The 11 temples here are

Vyagreswara Swamy, Vyagreswaram

Menakeswara Swamy, Pedapudi

Ananda Rameswara Swamy, Irusumanda

Visweswara Swamy, Vakkalanka

Chenna Malleswara Swamy, Nedunuru

Raghaveswara Swamy, Mukkamala

Bhogeswara Swamy, Mosalapalli

Chenna Malleswara Swamy, Palagummi

Veereswara Swamy, Gangalakurru Agraharam

Chenna Malleswara Swamy, Gangalakurru

Abhinava Vyaghreswara Swamy, Pulletikurru.

Devotees carry the Prabhalu(floats) representing the Ekadasha Rudras from these 11 temples in a procession.

These Prabhalu are decorated with flowers, clothes, the one from Vyagreswaram is considered the chief Rudra, while the one from Mosalapalli is the host here. These Prabhalu are carried through paddy fields,streams by devotees.

The Gangalakurru Agraharam Prabhalu of Veereswara Swamy is the final one to enter, and every one waits with folded hands to witness it.

Once these Prabhalu are hosted, they should not be put down on the ground again, and when they cross the Kausika River, the devotees ensure they don’t get wet. Around 20 people at a time carry them, thousands turn up to watch this sight.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!NDkI!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4867dda8-0739-4048-91d3-edf17379b9ab_1024x764.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!NDkI!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4867dda8-0739-4048-91d3-edf17379b9ab_1024x764.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

All these Prabhalu are hand crafted by the devotees themselves, and this tradition is being observed since the last 400 years by around 90 villages near to Jagannathota. It’s believed this was started by the Pithapuram Rajulu.

There are also smaller prabhalu made by children who carry them chanting name of Shiva, the stronger youth, carry the large Prabhalu on their shoulders with chants of Hara Hara Mahadeva, walking all the way with bare feet over paddy fields, lanes.

Finally the Prabhalu are immersed in the small lakes and ponds by devotees, chanting the name of Shiva, a sight to be witnessed.

This Jagganathota Prabhala Theertham was mentioned by Narendra Modi once , where he praised about it. Also in 2023 Republic Day float from Andhra Pradesh depicted this festival. The recent Telugu movie Naa Saami Ranga also had this as a backdrop.

While Jagannathota Prabhala Theertham is quite popular in Konaseema, it is one of the festivities that every one must know about and see at least once in their lifetime, the color, the celebrations, the Bhakti, all showing beauty of Sanathana Dharma.

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

5
